problem: Find the value of $h$ such that the following equation in base $h$ is true: $$\begin{array}{c@{}c@{}c@{}c@{}c@{}c} &&6&4&5&3_h\\ &+&7&5&1&2_h\\ \cline{2-6} &1&6&1&6&5_h.\\ \end{array}$$

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what is meant by "base $h$" in this equation?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many possible values of $h$ are there in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Why is it important to note that the two rightmost columns do not require carrying?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Why is there a residue of $1$ in the third column of the solution?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the process of subtracting $1$ from the sum of $4$ and $5$ and why it leads to the value of $h$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other examples where the concept of base is important in solving a problem?
classified_questions.label: e
